title: NGE being retrospective sorta passage of time related
display_title: NGE being retrospective sorta passage of time related
wikitext:
[]: NGE being retrospective (sorta passage of time-related).             Quote Post by Sailor Star Dust » Tue Apr 05, 2011 8:17 pm I could have sworn there was a thread discussing this sort of thing before (I don't believe it was the Final Scene Passage of Time thread, but maybe in there too) but my search-fu has failed me no matter how hard I looked. Somebody can feel free to merge or split/merge if they find what I'm talking about. Talk amongst yourselves, I'll give you a topic: Azathoth wrote: At least once that I can think of, NGE does suggest that at least part of the work is Shinji's retrospective: when Misato breaks down after Kaji's death, Shinji's comments on his failure to comfort her are in the past tense, including the line, "I was just a child" - which would almost seem suggest that the retrospective in question takes place after he attains adulthood in 26/26'.

[]:             Quote Post by FreakyFilmFan4ever » Tue Apr 05, 2011 9:06 pm I would equate this as being sort of the same story-telling technique as Rei's soliloquy in Episode 14, where even though she's not physically present in any more during the sequence she still manages to narrate a small bit if existentialism in symbolic form. The reason for this existentialistic view of herself is because she's more or less kept everyone else around her at bay. she mentions her interaction with other in her soliloquy, but since they're not often and the connection aren't as deep the focus remains mainly on herself and the world around her, with other people merely making up that world. In this case Shinji's narration sees to have the similar effect in that it's his thoughts on his interaction with others, but more specifically with Misato, who he's spent the entire TV series living with. It's more specific than Rei's narration in episode 14 because of the time he's spent with her. It's also not a poetic as Rei simply because Shinji doesn't really think in such abstract terms about himself or others.

[]:             Quote Post by Azathoth » Tue Apr 05, 2011 9:14 pm Shinji often (too often) internally reflects on his interactions with others, that's not what interested me about the scene in 21 - really, it's the use of past tense. Most of the time when Shinji voices over a monologue without moving his lips, we're given to understand that it's his current thoughts (duh). Yet the scene in 21 is explicitly not his current thoughts - it's his future thoughts, looking back over these events later. That's far from complete evidence that he's having these thoughts post-series, of course - especially since, after episode 21, the majority of his screentime is probably taken up by increasingly pitiful introspections. FWIW, though, the first time I watched the series, once Misato died in EoE, I thought "Oh, so that's what that meant" - interpreting the situation to mean that the 21 end monologue is meant to be triggered by Shinji's guilt over Misato's death in 25/25'.

[]:             Quote Post by gatotsu911 » Tue Apr 05, 2011 10:39 pm Ehhhhhhh, I don't buy it. The "I was still a child" moment is the one and only time in the series in which Shinji or anybody else offers any sort of Wonder Years -esque past-tense monologue, and I'm more ready to dismiss it as a fluke in the writing of that particular episode than an indicator of a radically different interpretation of the series. Shinji could be reflecting on this scene from any point in the future - the next episode, or even five minutes after the scene in question takes place, for all we know. There's really no indicator as to when this retrospective is taking place, and I really didn't detect any foreshadowing in that particular scene. Then again, this is Evangelion we're talking about. If that's how you see it, you can probably find tons of evidence to support your argument, and who's to say you're objectively wrong?

[]:             Quote Post by Sailor Star Dust » Tue Apr 05, 2011 10:51 pm gatotsu911 wrote: ( Happy Days is the show I'm thinking of, isn't it?)

[]:             Quote Post by gatotsu911 » Tue Apr 05, 2011 10:55 pm Fixed. Also, anyone interested in creating a Wonder Years version of Evangelion has my blessing and full cooperation.

[]:             Quote Post by Trajan » Wed Apr 06, 2011 6:46 pm I don't believe so. The thread's tangible piece of evidence for retrospection could simply be a mistranslation / script flub. Plus knowing how people use grammar in real life (from my experience anyway) Shinji could just be referring to "I was still a child" in the very recent past as in 'back in front of Misato's door I was still just a child.'

[]:             Quote Post by gwern » Sat Apr 09, 2011 11:15 am The 'a child' line makes little sense in context for this interpretation. From ADV's Platinum script for episode 21: EYECATCH B: EPISODE #21': HE WAS AWARE THAT HE WAS STILL A CHILD.

[]:             Quote Post by Eva Yojimbo » Sun Apr 10, 2011 3:47 am gatotsu911 wrote: anyone interested in creating a Wonder Years version of Evangelion has my blessing and full cooperation.

[]:             Quote Post by NemZ » Sun Apr 10, 2011 4:28 am Wait a sec... is Shinji saying that his cowardly reaction is why he realized he was still a child, or is he rather using his childhood as an excuse to rationalize his failure to act?

[]:             Quote Post by gwern » Sun Apr 10, 2011 10:24 am NemZ wrote: is Shinji saying that his cowardly reaction is why he realized he was still a child, or is he rather using his childhood as an excuse to rationalize his failure to act?
